$comment: Single source of truth for the RockCap file-naming standard (docs/classification/RockCap_FileNamingStandard_RC_INTERNAL_V1.2_20260708.md §9). Consumed by humans, the filename parser, and the classifier. Extend deliberately — DocTypes are a controlled list.

notes: Status and Version appear on RockCap-authored docs and any third-party doc that arrives in drafts/versions (valuations, QS and monitoring reports, legals); simple inbound docs omit them.
Filing date is ALWAYS the final token. Document date (dual-date types only) sits immediately after the DocType — never adjacent to the filing date.
No spaces, underscores, or full stops inside a field value.

parseOrder: documentDate: a \d{8} token immediately after the DocType (dual-date DocTypes only)
version/revision/status: recognised tokens (V\d+.\d+, R\d+, or a status word)
origin: the token that is 'RC' or begins with an origin role prefix
docType: the token after the Scheme

dates.filingDate: On every file, final token. When the file entered our filing as the current version (issue date for RC docs, received date for third-party). The recency marker.
dates.documentDate: Dual-date DocTypes only, immediately after the DocType. The date printed on / effective for the document itself — pins true vintage (an old planning permission can be received after a new one).

documentDateSource.PlanningPermission: permission/decision date; application date if unavailable

version.majorBump: client issues a NEW appraisal, or a material change lands: GDV moves, comps added, scheme scope changes

reissue.rule: A lender may send several term sheets, sometimes within the hour. Keep EVERY set, numbered R1, R2, R3… Never delete earlier ones — how terms changed is intelligence.

origins.RC: RockCap-authored. No individual initials — the app tracks who edited.
origins.CLIENT-: From the borrower/sponsor. Council consents, drawings and client-side reports arriving via the client are CLIENT-.
origins.LENDER-: From a funder. Canonical name aligned to the lender database (LENDER-Funding365, never F365).
origins.VALUER-: Only when the valuer delivered to RockCap directly.
origins.QS-: Quantity / monitoring surveyor, direct delivery only.
origins.SPV-: Borrowing vehicle if distinct from sponsor, direct delivery only.
origins.OTHER-: Direct delivery from any other party.

ClientBrief.note: no taxonomy equivalent — 'Lender Brief Note' is the lender-facing brief; a client-facing brief type doesn't exist yet

BuildCostSchedule.note: our costed schedule; nearest taxonomy value is 'Contract Sum Analysis' but that denotes the QS/contractor cost plan — no clear equivalent, needs an operator call

DeveloperBuildCosts.note: client's raw costs; same 'Contract Sum Analysis' ambiguity as BuildCostSchedule — no clear equivalent

AppraisalModel.note: RC-origin maps to RockCap Appraisal Model; CLIENT-origin to Client Land Appraisal

TermsAnalysis.note: confirmed against placement-rules (terms_analysis); 'Lender Comparison Table' / 'Lender Analysis Note' are sibling types for the same folder

AppendixA.note: comps master schedule — placement-rules documents 'Comparable Schedule' as the address-keyed Appendix A deliverable

PlanningPermission.note: NEVER abbreviate Drawing…/PlanningPermission to 'Plan/Plans' — design drawings and granted consent are different documents with different credit weight

S106Agreement.note: CORRECTED from tentative 'S106 Discharge/Variation' — that taxonomy type is explicitly the council's discharge/variation DECISION, 'distinct from the original S106 deed' (fileTypeDefinitions). The original deed files under the generic 'Planning Documentation' (its keywords include s106)

CILNotice.note: 'Planning Documentation' keywords include 'cil'; no CIL-specific taxonomy type exists

PlanningConditions.note: confirmed — the decision notice itself (conditions attached) is 'Planning Permission Decision Notice'; standalone conditions docs are generic planning documentation

TermsBundle.note: multiple lenders in one doc — origin LENDER-Multiple; no multi-lender bundle type in the taxonomy ('Indicative Terms' is per-lender)

EmailTerms.note: terms captured as an email/screenshot (PNG term sheets); vision-extracted text chunks by design

QSReport.note: no generic QS-report type — content resolves to 'Initial Monitoring Report' / 'Interim Monitoring Report' (monitoring surveys) or 'Contract Sum Analysis' (cost plan); can't pick one statically

FacilityAgreement.note: confirmed — 'Facility Letter' is the taxonomy's executed-facility type (placement: post_completion)

CertificateOfTitle.note: confirmed — the taxonomy's 'Report on Title' keywords/identification rules explicitly include 'Certificate of Title'

Warranty.note: taxonomy splits warranties into 'Collateral Warranty' and 'NHBC Warranty' — the generic token can't pick one; classifier decides from content

KYC.note: taxonomy holds specific KYC types (Passport, Driving License, Utility Bill, Application Form, Track Record, …) — no umbrella 'KYC' type; classifier picks the specific one

ID.note: resolves to 'Passport' or 'Driving License' by content — no generic ID type

CorporateStructure.note: no structure-chart type in the taxonomy ('Company Search' / 'Corporate Authorisations' are different documents)

Misc.note: temporary — raise to add the real type, don't invent one

$todo: Reconciliation against src/v4/lib/placement-rules.ts + convex/fileTypeDefinitions.ts DONE 2026-07-08 (all tentative flags resolved: 5 confirmed, S106Agreement corrected to 'Planning Documentation'; nulls filled where a clear taxonomy value exists). Remaining nulls are GENUINE taxonomy gaps (ClientBrief, BuildCostSchedule, DeveloperBuildCosts, TermsBundle, QSReport, Warranty, KYC, ID, CorporateStructure, Misc) — see each entry's note; do not invent taxonomy values, extend fileTypeDefinitions deliberately if one is needed.
